operating_state: Total revenues increased to $2.1 billion driven by rental revenue growth reflecting strong rent change of 53.7% on lease rollovers and same-store NOI growth of 5.9%. Net earnings attributable to common stockholders reached $592 million versus $584 million prior year, with consolidated occupancy declining to 94.8% from 95.4%. Strategic capital revenues grew driven by higher recurring fees and increased operating distributions from unconsolidated entities.
cost_structure: Real Estate Segment NOI expanded as rental expense increases were outpaced by revenue growth while Strategic Capital Segment NOI improved as expenses declined. Interest payments net of capitalized amounts increased to $269 million from $211 million reflecting higher borrowing costs, with G&A expenses increasing modestly and depreciation rising to $652 million.
material_events: Issued C$750 million of 8-year senior notes at 4.2% weighted average rate in February generating net proceeds for general corporate purposes and debt repayment. CEO Hamid Moghadam announced retirement effective January 1, 2026, with President Daniel Letter appointed as successor CEO and added to the Board. Completed 14 development stabilizations with build-to-suit composition and generated net proceeds from property dispositions. Common stock quarterly dividend increased to $1.01 from $0.96 per share.
macro_exposures: Customer decision-making anticipated to slow due to heightened economic uncertainty from tariff policy changes since early April, with supply chain disruption potentially creating repositioning opportunities. Foreign currency exchange impacts generated $55 million in unrealized losses versus $35 million in gains prior year across British pound, Canadian dollar, euro and Japanese yen investments. Interest rate sensitivity persists through variable-rate exposures and refinancing requirements.
forward_outlook: Maintaining disciplined capital deployment focused on build-to-suit development for logistics and data centers with development pipeline requiring remaining investment. Land bank and redevelopment sites support potential future development capacity. Lease mark-to-market of approximately 25% provides organic NOI growth runway despite market rent headwinds, with leadership transition to Letter as CEO signaling strategic continuity.
uncertainty_profile: Tariff policy volatility creates unpredictable impacts on business operations and customer behavior patterns with development portfolio completion timeline extending through July 2027. Debt maturities require refinancing execution in 2025 and 2026 while unconsolidated co-investment ventures carry third-party debt requiring future refinancing coordination amid foreign currency translation volatility.
